steps.title: Determine the equation of line h from the graph
steps.content:
Examining the graph, line $h$ has a steep negative slope. Counting grid squares: moving right 1 unit causes the line to drop approximately 10 units vertically. The slope is −10.

The line crosses the y-axis at $y = -8$ (between the labeled marks at −10 and the x-axis). So line $h$ is:
$$y = -10x - 8$$
or in standard form:
$$10x + y = -8$$

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines condition for no solution
steps.content:
For system with no solution, lines must be parallel (same slope, different intercepts).

Line $k$: $sx + 40y = t$ must have the same slope as line $h$.

For parallel lines, coefficient ratios must match:
$$\frac{s}{10} = \frac{40}{1}$$
Therefore: $s = 400$

Line $k$ becomes $400x + 40y = t$. Dividing by 40 to match the form of line $h$:
$$10x + y = \frac{t}{40}$$

steps.title: Identify which t value breaks the no-solution condition
steps.content:
Line $h$ is $10x + y = -8$ and line $k$ (with $s = 400$) is $10x + y = \frac{t}{40}$.

For the system to have no solution, the lines must be distinct:
$$\frac{t}{40} \neq -8$$
$$t \neq -320$$

This means all values of $t$ except $−320$ result in a system with no solution. If $t = -320$, the two equations become identical (infinitely many solutions), violating the no-solution condition. Therefore, $t = -320$ is NOT a possible value. That matches choice D — D is correct.
